hypothesis: A nonlinear quadratic GF(2) cocycle built from complete factor-count parities might distinguish terminal output pair 00 after rational additive cocycles fail.
interpretation: Exact coefficient elimination shows that every degree-at-most-two polynomial in the parities of all inverse-word factors through range three has constant terminal-pair potential in any all-word one-step cocycle identity. This remains true with an arbitrary potential on the exact current fringe state, so this nonlinear finite-memory class cannot distinguish finite support.
limitations: covers algebraic degree at most two only
covers factor-count parities of factors of length at most three only
does not cover observables depending on factor arrangement beyond global counts
does not cover growing memory or range increasing with strip width
does not prove the alternating survivor has infinite support
does not exclude eventual center period two
does not solve Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
proof_scope: The finite GF(2) systems are coefficient proofs of all-word no-go statements: any proposed identity in the stated class must satisfy the finite subsystem, whose exact row space contains every terminal-potential difference V_s+V_11. The analyzer independently cross-checks factor parities by direct and streaming implementations and freezes exact row counts and ranks.
question: problem1

exact_conclusions.actual_orbit_scope: allowing an arbitrary potential on the exact current fringe state only changes the branch equation by a scalar and therefore does not evade the fixed-branch theorem
exact_conclusions.nonlinear_no_go: quadratic parity functionals of factor counts through range three cannot distinguish terminal 00 from a nonzero output pair
exact_conclusions.remaining_target: higher algebraic degree, factor range at least four, genuinely growing memory, or a non-factor-based nonlinear observable

ranges.exact_conclusion: every degree-at-most-two GF(2) polynomial in factor-count parities of this range has terminal-pair potential constant in any all-word one-step cocycle identity
